tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows severe metabolic acidosis (arterial pH 6.9), markedly elevated creatinine (2.8 mg/dL), very high total bilirubin (10.1 mg/dL), and abnormal urine findings (protein, nitrite, ketones, glucose). These suggest acute kidney and liver dysfunction, possible sepsis, and metabolic decompensation. Immediate clinical evaluation and intervention are urgently recommended.

text.value: Tom Hayward

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
